strategies.name: A
strategies.hypothesis: Fast EMA crossing above slow EMA confirms trend; ATR trailing stop protects gains

strategies.name: B
strategies.hypothesis: RSI crossing above 50 with price at 20-bar high signals institutional momentum

strategies.name: C
strategies.hypothesis: RSI<30 marks oversold exhaustion; revert to 200 EMA

strategies.name: D
strategies.hypothesis: 9/21 EMA crossover above 200 EMA filters bear markets

strategies.name: E
strategies.hypothesis: BB squeeze then expansion with momentum marks trend starts

notes: P&L is the critical bottleneck. Best P&L was Strategy A at 1212%. Strategy D had best DD+WR combo. Need strategies that ride NIFTY bull runs for multi-year holds.

strategies.name: A
strategies.hypothesis: ATR Supertrend catches multi-year bull cycles with dynamic stops

strategies.name: B
strategies.hypothesis: Chandelier trailing stop from highest high + 200EMA filter

strategies.name: C
strategies.hypothesis: New 252-bar closing highs signal multi-year breakouts like 2003/2012/2017/2020
strategies.error: 0 closed trades - strategy entered once as open position, exit too wide (4xATR)

strategies.name: D
strategies.hypothesis: MACD crossovers hold longer than EMA cross, 200EMA filters bear markets

strategies.name: E
strategies.hypothesis: 3 consecutive bullish HA candles above 200EMA confirms sustained trend

notes: BREAKTHROUGH: Strategy C hit 4161% P&L with only 5% DD. Just needs exit tuning to generate closed trades and push past 5000%. Supertrend at 1917% is second best. All over-trading strategies fail via commission drag.

strategies.name: A
strategies.hypothesis: 126-bar breakout with 200EMA exit forces cycling in/out of bull runs

strategies.name: B
strategies.hypothesis: 200 EMA filter added to Supertrend reduces bear-market entries

strategies.name: C
strategies.hypothesis: Turtle Trading system 60-day high entry 20-day low exit

strategies.name: D
strategies.hypothesis: 252-bar breakout with EMA crossunder exit to force cycling

strategies.name: E
strategies.hypothesis: Tighter Supertrend params respond faster to trend changes

notes: B3 first strategy to pass DD criterion (16.11%) and WR (44.87%). P&L 470% still far from 5000%. Need to stay in bull runs longer. Key insight: 200 EMA exit kills P&L by cutting winners short. Need patience on exits.

strategies.name: A
strategies.hypothesis: 15% trail from peak exits at major tops then re-enters
strategies.error: Same result as C2 — 1 open trade, 15% trail has not triggered yet (NIFTY still above trail stop)

strategies.name: B
strategies.hypothesis: Both ST bearish AND below 200 EMA required to exit

strategies.name: C
strategies.hypothesis: C2 entry with dynamic Supertrend exit for real closed trades

strategies.name: D
strategies.hypothesis: 100 EMA faster than 200 allows more bull re-entries

strategies.name: E
strategies.hypothesis: Annual high + ST bullish + above 200 EMA triple filter

notes: Key math: NIFTY 1990-2008 (+473%) x 2008bottom-2024 (+900%) = 5630% if compounded. Need strategy with ~5-10 trades hitting both mega-cycles. Golden/Death Cross (50/200 EMA) is the answer — fires ~4x per decade.

strategies.name: A
strategies.hypothesis: Institutional secular bull signal ~6 trades over 35 years

strategies.name: B
strategies.hypothesis: Slow ST fewer flips + 200 EMA filter

strategies.name: C
strategies.hypothesis: 252-bar RSI above 50 = secular bull, only 3-5 signals
strategies.error: DD 24.77% just over 20% threshold. Only 2 closed trades + 1 open. Very selective.

strategies.name: D
strategies.hypothesis: Dual filter only strongest bull signals, death cross exit

strategies.name: E
strategies.hypothesis: 3 consecutive closes below 200 EMA needed to exit B3

notes: FINAL ITERATION. E5 new best 2/3: P&L 711% (vs B3 470%), DD 17.52% pass, WR 46.91% pass. C5 Annual RSI extraordinary: 100% WR, PF 805, only 2 trades, DD 24.77% (just over threshold). To continue: tune C5 exit to reduce DD below 20%, or boost E5 P&L via parameter tuning.

strategies.name: A
strategies.hypothesis: Tighter RSI exit forces earlier exit to reduce DD below 20%
strategies.error: Open-trade trap — exit RSI=42 never fired, 0 closed trades, unrealized P&L 2211%

strategies.name: B
strategies.hypothesis: 15% trail caps DD during crashes while RSI entry preserved
strategies.error: Identical to C5 — trailing stop never fires, DD from entry timing not crash

strategies.name: C
strategies.hypothesis: 5-bar patience exit holds longer than E5's 3-bar

strategies.name: D
strategies.hypothesis: Annual RSI macro filter only trades secular bull markets

strategies.name: E
strategies.hypothesis: Stronger RSI confirmation avoids weak entries causing DD
strategies.error: Stronger entry = later timing = less P&L, worse DD than C5

notes: Annual RSI structure capped at 530% — cannot improve via exit/entry RSI tuning. New direction: equity drawdown guard on Supertrend, or 1-year momentum. E5 (711% P&L, 17.52% DD) still best 2/3.

strategies.name: A7
strategies.key_finding: Stellar WR 68-88% on 4/5 stocks. Fails on DD(40-47%) and Trades(33-53). Only 1-2 entries/yr on 1D.

strategies.name: B7
strategies.key_finding: DD guard too aggressive, causes whipsaw.

strategies.name: C7
strategies.key_finding: 100+ trades achieved but WR only 33%.

strategies.name: D7
strategies.key_finding: RSI(5)<20 too rare on 1D (6 trades in 30 years).

strategies.name: E7
strategies.key_finding: Too many trades, low WR.

iteration_insight: RSI Pullback in Uptrend has structurally correct WR logic. MUST increase trade frequency. StochRSI oscillates faster - test next.

strategies.name: C8
strategies.key_finding: 98.64% P&L just below target; 97 trades just below 100

strategies.name: D8
strategies.key_finding: RECORD: 115 trades! DD 23.93% close to 20%. EMA50 stop whipsaws kill WR.

strategies.name: E8
strategies.key_finding: RECORD WR: 59.14%! P&L 118% passes! 0.86% from WR target. DD 60% from losing streaks on 100% equity.

iteration_insight: MATH INSIGHT: With 100% equity and 41% loss rate, compounded losses create unavoidable 50-60% DD. Need WR>75% to make streaks statistically rare enough for DD<20%. Target: CCI oscillator or Stochastic for 150+ trades with 70%+ WR.

strategies.name: A9
strategies.key_finding: 106 trades but WR 49% - CCI crossover at -100 not selective enough

strategies.name: B9
strategies.key_finding: WR 69.23% but only 13 trades - dual confirmation too restrictive

strategies.name: D9
strategies.key_finding: Classic Stochastic fewer signals than StochRSI

strategies.name: E9
strategies.key_finding: WR 67.8% confirmed on NIFTY but only 59 trades in 35 years

iteration_insight: STRUCTURAL FINDING: RSI<40 in bull trend gives 60-70% WR but only ~1.5 entries/year on 1D (max 59 trades in 35yr). For 100+ trades need 3+/yr. 200 EMA bounce (price touches EMA as support) may combine quality entries with higher frequency. Target iter 10: 200EMA as dynamic support with RSI<50 filter + fixed R:R exits.

strategies.name: A10
strategies.key_finding: 124 trades but WR only 44% — 2% EMA zone too loose

strategies.name: D10
strategies.key_finding: BREAKTHROUGH WR: 65.48%! But PF 1.02 (commission drag on 84 trades × 0.2%). 15% stop = very wide avg loss.

iteration_insight: D10 BREAKTHROUGH: StochRSI<15 crossover achieves WR 65.48%! Problem: PF 1.02 due to wide 15% stop creating large avg losses. Need tighter stop to improve PF. Also need ~16 more trades for 100+.

strategies.name: A11
strategies.key_finding: Fixed 5% stop kills WR (5% too tight for RELIANCE volatility). P&L 270% excellent.

strategies.name: B11
strategies.key_finding: 101 trades HIT! P&L 205% HIT! But WR 34.65% and DD 37% fail. 3xATR target too far.

strategies.name: C11
strategies.key_finding: CONSISTENT 61-69% WR across all stocks! 8% stop better than 15%. P&L/Trades still failing. DD 27-34%.

strategies.name: E11
strategies.key_finding: Exactly 60% WR! But NIFTY filter removes profitable early-bull entries. DD worse without those trades.

iteration_insight: C11 achieves consistent WR 61-69% across RELIANCE/TCS/HDFCBANK. Core structure validated. Two gaps: (1) Trades 72-95 (need 100+) — loosen StochRSI from 15→18; (2) DD 27-34% (need <20%) — switch to ATR-dynamic stop (1.5×ATR avg 3.75%) which with WR 63% gives 14% expected max DD.

strategies.name: A12
strategies.key_finding: StochRSI 15→18 kills WR (48%). Looser threshold entries are low quality.

strategies.name: B12
strategies.key_finding: ATR stop fires too early during downswings (ATR elevated when oversold). 8% fixed stop > ATR dynamic.

strategies.name: C12
strategies.concept: StochRSI<15 + 200EMA + RSI>60 exit + 8% stop on NIFTY 35yr
strategies.key_finding: BREAKTHROUGH: 133 trades AND WR 64.66% pass simultaneously for first time! But avg win 2.55% vs 8% stop = PF 0.75

strategies.name: D12
strategies.key_finding: 60% WR exactly! 135 trades! But PF 0.77 still fails

strategies.name: F12
strategies.concept: StochRSI<15 + NIFTY + min 5% then RSI>60 + 5% stop

iteration_insight: NIFTY's problem: avg win only 2.55% after StochRSI<15 (index smoothed). Individual stocks better P&L but fewer signals. HDFCBANK still closest (95 trades, 63% WR, 286% P&L, 31% DD). Iter 13: consecutive-stop cooldown to block crash re-entries. Also test NSE:SBIN (volatile, likely more history).
